prs.title: feat(service-worker): ensure fire() inherits default 404 fallback behavior (#4632)
prs.body:
This PR restores consistency to the `fire()` function in the Service Worker adapter. Currently, `fire()` disables the 404 network fallback by default, which contradicts its documentation and the behavior of the manual `handle()` function.

### Changes:
- Updated `fire()` in `src/adapter/service-worker/index.ts` to use the default `HandleOptions` (which enables 404 fallback).
- Improved JSDoc in `src/adapter/service-worker/handler.ts` to clearly document the fallback fetch behavior.
- Added unit tests to verify that `fire()` correctly triggers a fallback fetch when a route is missing.

Resolves #4632

prs.title: feat(basic-auth): add passUsername option to support context injection (#4192)
prs.body:
This PR adds a `passUsername` option to the `basicAuth` middleware, allowing the authenticated username to be automatically passed to the context. This addresses a highly requested feature for accessing the username in subsequent route handlers without re-parsing headers.

### Changes:
- Added `passUsername?: boolean` to `BasicAuthOptions`.
- Implemented automatic context injection: `c.set('basicAuthUsername', username)`.
- Added module augmentation for `ContextVariableMap` to ensure type-safety.
- Updated JSDoc with examples.
- Added comprehensive tests in `src/middleware/basic-auth/index.test.ts`.

Resolves #4192

prs.title: docs: add hono-method-override to ecosystem snippets
prs.body:
Resolves #195.

As requested by [@yusukebe in #195 (comment)](https://github.com/honojs/website/issues/195#issuecomment-1882468227), I have added the `hono-method-override` plugin to the 3rd-party Middleware examples list. I created a brief markdown page demonstrating its usage and linked to the repository.

prs.title: docs: add warning about importing Vue code into nitro
prs.body:
Resolves #30862.

Added an `::important` callout explicitly warning against importing Vue app code (components, composables) into the Nitro `server/` directory to prevent severe IDE slowdowns due to TypeScript "type leaking". This explains why the `shared/` directory distinction exists and prevents bad DX for newcomers.

prs.title: docs: clarify overlapping dynamic route parameters limitation
prs.body:
Fixes #3518

### Description
Adds a warning note to the Dynamic Routing documentation clarifying that overlapping dynamic routes with different parameter names in the same directory (e.g., `[id].post.ts` and `[slug].get.ts`) are not currently supported due to router limitations.

This addresses the documentation request brought up by the maintainer in #3518 where developers were encountering unexpected behavior with overlapping parameters.

prs.title: docs: fix undefined variable host used in Drizzle connection snippet
prs.body:
Fixes #43920

## What kind of change does this PR introduce?
- Documentation update

## What is the current behavior?
The Drizzle ORM connection snippet uses an undeclared/undefined `host` variable for string manipulation. 

## What is the new behavior?
Replaces `host` with `connectionString` per the issue's suggestion, effectively fixing the `ReferenceError: host is not defined` that developers encounter when copying the snippet.

prs.title: docs: clarify transaction rollback change tracking
prs.body:
Fixes #16001

This PR clarifies the documentation for `Connection#transaction()` to explain *why* fields are marked as modified after a transaction rollback. This directly addresses the confusion raised in issue #16001, where a user asked if Mongoose marking fields as modified (rather than unmodified) was a mistake.

The new wording makes it clear that keeping fields tracked as modified ensures any subsequent `save()` attempts will still persist those changes to the database.

prs.title: docs: Clarify environment variables across packages dependency behavior
prs.body:
Fixes #11340

### Description
Added a missing section in the "Using environment variables" guide explaining how environment variables from dependency packages affect cache hashes and executing apps.

This clarifies the two main scenarios:
1. When an app depends on a package's task (via `dependsOn`), cache invalidation cascades automatically.
2. When an app directly imports variables at runtime/build-time without a task dependency, the app must declare the environment variable in its own `turbo.json`.

This directly answers the question raised by the community in #11340.

prs.title: docs: clarify Baseline widely available description
prs.body:
## Description

Fixes #21144

The phrase "These are browsers that were released at least 2.5 years ago" was backwards and confusing, implying we support browsers released e.g. 5+ years ago. The correct meaning of [Baseline Widely Available](https://web-platform-dx.github.io/web-features/) is that it only uses features which have been available across major browsers for at least 2.5 years.

This PR updates the wording to match the user's suggestion in the issue for clarity.

## Changes
- Updated `docs/guide/index.md` wording to accurately reflect the 2.5 year feature availability window.

prs.title: fix(a11y): align menuButtonLabel with visible "Options" text (WCAG 2.…
prs.body:
## Problem

Fixes #24304

The chart context menu button (visible label: **"Options"**) has its `aria-label` derived from the `lang.accessibility.exporting.menuButtonLabel` string, which was ''View chart menu, {chartTitle}''. Since this accessible name does not start with the visible label text "Options", it violates **WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name)**, causing screen readers (like Windows Narrator) to announce a name that doesn't match what sighted users see.

**Actual:** Screen reader announces _"View chart menu"_
**Expected:** Screen reader should announce _"Options"_ (matching the visible label)

## Changes

**File:** `ts/Accessibility/Options/LangDefaults.ts`

```diff
- menuButtonLabel: 'View chart menu, {chartTitle}'
+ menuButtonLabel: 'Options, {chartTitle}'
```

The new string starts with "Options" - matching the visible button text - which satisfies WCAG 2.5.3. The `{chartTitle}` context is preserved for additional clarity for screen reader users.

## Impact

- **docs/a11y change only** - one string in the lang defaults.
- No functional logic changed.
- Fixes the mismatch reported by the Windows Narrator with Accessibility Insights.

prs.title: fix(grid): correct broken credits demo URL in JSDoc
prs.body:
## Problem

Fixes #24326

The JSDoc comment on the `Options.credits` property in `CreditsProComposition.ts` contains a `@link` pointing to a non-existent demo URL:

```
https://jsfiddle.net/gh/get/library/pure/highcharts/highcharts/tree/master/samples/grid-pro/credits
```

This URL returns a 404 error, as shown in the [API docs](https://api.highcharts.com/grid/credits).

## Fix

Updated the URL to point to the correct existing sample:

```
https://jsfiddle.net/gh/get/library/pure/highcharts/highcharts/tree/master/samples/grid-pro/e2e/credits-pro
```

## Changes

- **File:** `ts/Grid/Pro/Credits/CreditsProComposition.ts`
- - **Change:** One-line URL fix in a JSDoc `@link` comment

prs.title: docs(exception): complete app.onError example with non-HTTPException fallback
docs(nodejs): clarify serveStatic root path resolution
prs.body:
## Problem

Fixes #4442

The current `serveStatic` documentation shows `root: './'` as the example, but this resolves relative to `process.cwd()` — the directory from which the Node.js process was started. In real-world projects where the server is run from a directory other than where the source file lives (e.g., monorepos, Docker containers, or when using a process manager), this causes 404 errors because the path resolves incorrectly.

Users have to discover through trial and error that they need to use `fileURLToPath(new URL('./', import.meta.url))` to get a path relative to the source file itself.

## Changes

- Added a VitePress `warning` callout block after the basic `root: './'` example in `docs/getting-started/nodejs.md`
- The warning explains **why** relative paths can be unreliable (they use `process.cwd()`)
- Provides the recommended `import.meta.url` pattern as a reliable alternative

## Impact

- Docs-only change. No source code modified.
- Helps users avoid an undocumented gotcha that wastes significant debugging time (as reported in issue #4442)

prs.title: Fix: error when onMouseEnter or onMouseLeave is undefined #6968
prs.body:
Hey! While working with the Line component, I noticed it crashes if you pass `undefined` to `onMouseEnter` or `onMouseLeave`. It happens because `adaptEventHandlers` tries to wrap and call anything starting with 'on' without checking if it's actually a function.

This fix just adds a quick `typeof` check in `src/util/types.ts` to make sure we only wrap actual functions. This makes it safer for common React patterns where you might want to disable a handler by passing undefined.

I've also added a small test file in `test/util/adaptEventHandlers.spec.ts` just to be sure it doesn't break again.

Fixes #6968

prs.title: Add missing zMin and zMax API documentation for packed bubble charts
prs.body:
Fixes #24135

## Summary

The official [packed bubble demo](https://www.highcharts.com/demo/highcharts/packed-bubble-split) uses `zMin` and `zMax` properties to control the Z value range, but these properties weren't showing up in the [API documentation](https://api.highcharts.com/highcharts/plotOptions.packedbubble) because they were incorrectly excluded.

## Changes Made

**File modified:** `ts/Series/PackedBubble/PackedBubbleSeriesDefaults.ts`

1. **Removed `zMin` and `zMax` from the `@excluding` directive** (line 36)
   - These properties were being excluded from the API docs even though they're fully functional and used in official demos
   
2. **Added comprehensive API documentation** for both properties (after line 74):
   - Detailed descriptions explaining their purpose
   - Cross-references between `zMin` and `zMax`
   - Links to the bubble chart demo showing them in action
   - Proper `@since`, `@type`, and `@product` tags
   - Followed the same documentation pattern as `BubbleSeries`

## Why This Matters

Users looking at the official demo saw `zMin` and `zMax` being used but couldn't find them in the API docs. This fix ensures the documentation accurately reflects what's available in the code.

## Testing

The properties already work in the code (as shown in the demo). This PR only adds the missing documentation - no functional changes to the codebase.

prs.title: Fix vertical alignment of text and icons in status bar

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Work not yet planned for a specific release.

prs.body:
This PR fixes the vertical alignment issue in the status bar where icons and text were not perfectly centered (off by 1-2 pixels), which was most noticeable on Windows 11 and macOS.

### Changes Made

Changed the .statusbar-item CSS class in statusbarpart.css from using inline-block to flexbox layout:

- Changed display: inline-block to display: flex
- - Added align-items: center for proper vertical centering  
- - Removed hardcoded line-height: 22px (no longer needed with flexbox)
- - Removed vertical-align: top (not applicable to flex items)
The child .statusbar-item-label already had flexbox with align-items: center, but the parent's inline-block was preventing proper vertical alignment. This fix ensures all status bar items (icons and text) are perfectly centered vertically.

### Testing

Verified the changes work correctly across different zoom levels and that all status bar elements (errors, warnings, branch indicator, sync icon, etc.) are properly aligned.

Fixes #162995

prs.title: Fix #24128: Prevent hidden series from creating gaps in column grouping
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes #24128 by adding a visibility check in `getColumnMetrics()` to prevent hidden series from reserving space in column layout calculations.

## Problem
When grouping was enabled in column charts, large horizontal gaps appeared between visible columns. This occurred because hidden series were still being counted in the `columnCount` calculation, causing the spacing algorithm to allocate space for invisible columns.

## Solution
Added `otherSeries.visible &&` check on line 272 of `ColumnSeries.ts` before counting series in the column metrics calculation. This ensures only visible series contribute to column width and positioning calculations.

## Testing
- Verified fix resolves the reported issue with hidden series
- Existing grouped and stacked column behavior remains unchanged for visible series

## Related Issue

prs.title: Feature: Optional smooth scrolling for message navigation

labels.name: not ready
labels.description: Needs work to be ready for maintainer review.

prs.body:
# Pull Request: Add Optional Smooth Scrolling for Message Navigation

## Description

This PR implements an optional smooth scrolling feature for message navigation in Zulip, addressing issue #37565.

### Problem

Currently, Zulip uses a "jump" navigation style when moving between messages with keyboard shortcuts (up/down arrows). While this is efficient for power users, many users coming from other platforms find it disorienting and would prefer a smoother, more familiar scrolling experience.

### Solution

This PR adds a new user preference setting `enable_smooth_scroll_navigation` that allows users to toggle between jump-style and smooth-scroll navigation modes.

**When smooth scrolling is enabled:**
1. If the next/previous message is already fully visible on screen → Just changes focus (same as current behavior)
2. If the next/previous message is not fully visible → Scrolls smoothly to bring it into view

**When smooth scrolling is disabled (default):**
- Behavior is identical to the current implementation

### Implementation Details

#### Backend Changes

1. **`zerver/models/users.py`**
   - Added `enable_smooth_scroll_navigation` field to `UserBaseSettings` model
   - Added to `modern_settings` dictionary for proper serialization

2. **`zerver/migrations/0773_add_smooth_scroll_navigation.py`**
   - Database migration to add the new field to both `UserProfile` and `RealmUserDefault` models
   - Default value: `False` (maintains backward compatibility)

#### Frontend Changes

3. **`web/src/user_settings.ts`**
   - Added `enable_smooth_scroll_navigation` to the user settings schema

4. **`web/src/navigate.ts`**
   - Added `is_message_fully_visible()` helper function
   - Modified `up()` function to support smooth scrolling
   - Modified `down()` function to support smooth scrolling
   - Uses existing `scroll_util.scroll_element_into_container()` for smooth scrolling

5. **`web/src/settings_config.ts`**
   - Added user-facing label: "Enable smooth scrolling when navigating messages with keyboard"

6. **`web/templates/settings/preferences_navigation.hbs`**
   - Added checkbox in Settings → Preferences → Navigation

### Testing

**Manual testing performed:**
- ✅ Enabled smooth scrolling and verified smooth scrolling behavior
- ✅ Verified immediate focus change when messages are fully visible
- ✅ Tested with long messages (page up/down still works correctly)
- ✅ Tested with short messages
- ✅ Disabled smooth scrolling and verified jump-style navigation is restored

**Browser compatibility:**
- Should be tested in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari

### Design Decisions

1. **Default to disabled**: Maintains backward compatibility and doesn't surprise existing users who are accustomed to jump-style navigation.

2. **Reuse existing scroll utilities**: Uses `scroll_util.scroll_element_into_container()` which already handles edge cases and provides smooth scrolling.

3. **Preserve long message handling**: The existing logic for handling long messages (using page up/down) takes precedence over smooth scrolling.

4. **Check full visibility**: Only scrolls when the target message is not fully visible, avoiding unnecessary scrolling when the message is already in view.

### Fixes

Fixes #37565

---

## Checklist

- [x] Backend model changes
- [x] Database migration
- [x] Frontend TypeScript changes
- [x] Settings UI changes
- [x] User-facing labels
- [x] Commit message follows Zulip conventions
- [ ] Tests added/updated (manual testing performed)
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed)

## Screenshots

(Screenshots would be added here showing the new setting in the preferences UI)

## Additional Notes

This implementation maintains all existing keyboard shortcut functionality while providing an optional smoother navigation experience. The feature is completely opt-in and doesn't affect users who prefer the current jump-style navigation.

prs.title: Fix quota exceeded warning for Copilot Pro usersFix/copilot pro quota warnings
prs.body:
# Fix Quota Exceeded Warning for Copilot Pro Users

## Description

Fixes #280694

This PR fixes an issue where Copilot Pro subscribers were not seeing quota exceeded warnings in the status bar when they exceeded their premium chat quota.

## Problem

The quota exceeded warning in the status bar was only being displayed for Free tier users (`ChatEntitlement.Free`), but Pro/ProPlus/Business/Enterprise users also have quotas:

- Pro users have 300 premium chat requests per month
- The quota data is properly fetched and stored in `chatEntitlementService.quotas`
- However, the status bar wasn't showing quota warnings for Pro users

## Root Cause

In `chatStatusEntry.ts`, the quota check had this condition:

```typescript
// Free Quota Exceeded
else if (this.chatEntitlementService.entitlement === ChatEntitlement.Free && (chatQuotaExceeded || completionsQuotaExceeded)) {
```

This meant Pro users would never see quota warnings in the status bar, even when they exceeded their premium chat quota.

## Solution

1. **Removed the Free-tier-only restriction** - The quota check now applies to all entitled users
2. **Added premium chat quota checking** - Pro users have a `premiumChat` quota that wasn't being checked
3. **Updated quota warning logic** - Now handles all three quota types: `chat`, `completions`, and `premiumChat`

## Changes

Updated `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatStatus/chatStatusEntry.ts`:

- Added `premiumChatQuotaExceeded` variable to check premium chat quota
- Changed condition from `ChatEntitlement.Free && (chatQuotaExceeded || completionsQuotaExceeded)` to `chatQuotaExceeded || completionsQuotaExceeded || premiumChatQuotaExceeded`
- Added specific warning message for premium chat quota: "Premium chat quota reached"
- Updated comment from "Free Quota Exceeded" to "Quota Exceeded (Free, Pro, ProPlus, Business, Enterprise)"

## Testing

This change affects the status bar display logic for quota warnings. All entitled users (Free, Pro, ProPlus, Business, Enterprise) will now see appropriate quota warnings when they exceed any of their quotas.

The existing entitlement service tests already verify quota calculation logic, so no new tests are needed for this UI display change.

## Impact

- Pro/ProPlus/Business/Enterprise users will now see quota warnings when they exceed their limits
- Free users will continue to see quota warnings as before
- No breaking changes to existing functionality
<!-- Thank you for submitting a Pull Request. Please:
* Read our Pull Request guidelines:
  https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute#pull-requests
* Associate an issue with the Pull Request.
* Ensure that the code is up-to-date with the `main` branch.
* Include a description of the proposed changes and how to test them.
-->

prs.title: Fix quota exceeded warning for Copilot Pro users
prs.body:
## Description

Fixes #280694

This PR fixes an issue where Copilot Pro subscribers were incorrectly seeing "out of requests" messages or not seeing quota warnings when they should.

### Problem

The quota exceeded warning in the status bar was only being displayed for Free tier users (`ChatEntitlement.Free`), but Pro/ProPlus/Business/Enterprise users also have quotas:
- Pro users have 300 premium chat requests per month
- The quota data is properly fetched and stored in `chatEntitlementService.quotas`
- However, the status bar wasn't showing quota warnings for Pro users

### Root Cause

In `chatStatusEntry.ts`, the quota check had this condition:

```typescript
// Free Quota Exceeded
else if (this.chatEntitlementService.entitlement === ChatEntitlement.Free && (chatQuotaExceeded || completionsQuotaExceeded)) {
```

This meant Pro users would never see quota warnings in the status bar, even when they exceeded their premium chat quota.

### Solution

1. **Removed the Free-tier-only restriction** - The quota check now applies to all entitled users
2. **Added premium chat quota checking** - Pro users have a `premiumChat` quota that wasn't being checked
3. **Updated quota warning logic** - Now handles all three quota types: `chat`, `completions`, and `premiumChat`

### Changes

- Updated `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatStatus/chatStatusEntry.ts`:
  - Added `premiumChatQuotaExceeded` variable to check premium chat quota
  - Changed condition from `ChatEntitlement.Free && (chatQuotaExceeded || completionsQuotaExceeded)` to `chatQuotaExceeded || completionsQuotaExceeded || premiumChatQuotaExceeded`
  - Added specific warning message for premium chat quota: "Premium chat quota reached"
  - Updated comment from "Free Quota Exceeded" to "Quota Exceeded (Free, Pro, ProPlus, Business, Enterprise)"

### Testing

This change affects the status bar display logic for quota warnings. All entitled users (Free, Pro, ProPlus, Business, Enterprise) will now see appropriate quota warnings when they exceed any of their quotas.

The existing entitlement service tests already verify quota calculation logic, so no new tests are needed for this UI display change.

### Impact

- **Pro/ProPlus/Business/Enterprise users** will now see quota warnings when they exceed their limits
- **Free users** will continue to see quota warnings as before
- No breaking changes to existing functionality

Remove obsolete maybeMigrateCurrentSession method
prs.body:
# Remove Obsolete Session Migration Code

## Summary

This PR removes the obsolete `maybeMigrateCurrentSession()` method and related code that was used to migrate chat sessions from the now-removed edits view to the unified chat view.

## Background

VS Code previously had a separate "edits view" for chat sessions. When that view was removed and unified into the main chat view, a migration path was created to move users' sessions over. The `maybeMigrateCurrentSession()` method handled this one-time migration.

Since sufficient time has passed and the edits view no longer exists, this migration code is no longer necessary and can be safely removed.

## Changes

This PR removes:

1. **`maybeMigrateCurrentSession()` method** (lines 121-144, 27 lines total)
   - Checked for old edits view memento
   - Migrated sessionId to unified view
   - Copied input text and mode settings
   - Set migration completion flag

2. **`hasMigratedCurrentSession` flag** from `IChatViewPaneState` interface
   - Used to track whether migration had already happened
   - No longer needed since migration is obsolete

3. **Method call** from constructor (line 116)
   - Called `this.maybeMigrateCurrentSession()` during initialization
   - Removed since method no longer exists

## Testing

- Code compiles without errors
- No other code references these removed items
- Migration logic was one-time and no longer relevant

## Fixes

#280040

prs.title: Export PieSectorShapeProps type
prs.body:
# Export PieSectorShapeProps Type

## Summary

This PR makes the `PieSectorShapeProps` type publicly available, improving the developer experience when working with custom Pie chart shapes.

## Problem

Currently, when developers want to create custom shape functions for Pie components, they need to manually recreate the `PieSectorShapeProps` type by adding the `isActive` property to `PieSectorDataItem`. This is error-prone and requires developers to inspect the internal implementation to understand the shape of the props.

```typescript
// Before: Developers had to manually type this
type MyPieShapeProps = PieSectorDataItem & { isActive: boolean; index: number };

const customShape = (props: MyPieShapeProps) => {
  // custom implementation
};
```

## Solution

This PR exports the `PieSectorShapeProps` type, allowing developers to use it directly:

```typescript
// After: Developers can import and use the official type
import { PieSectorShapeProps } from 'recharts';

const customShape = (props: PieSectorShapeProps) => {
  // custom implementation
};
```

## Changes

1. **src/polar/Pie.tsx** - Added `export` keyword to the `PieSectorShapeProps` type definition (line 163)
2. **src/index.ts** - Added `PieSectorShapeProps` to the list of exported types from `./polar/Pie` (line 56)

## Testing

- ✅ Type definitions build successfully (`npm run build-types`)
- ✅ No breaking changes to existing code
- ✅ The type is now available for import by consumers

## Benefits

- Improved developer experience
- Better type safety
- Reduced code duplication
- Clearer API surface

## Fixes

#6705


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **API Updates**
  * Made `PieSectorShapeProps` type publicly available for use in Pie component implementations.
  * Exposed `RadialBarProps` type for RadialBar component integration.
  * Enhanced TypeScript support for developers using Polar chart components.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>

prs.title: Fix NullPointerException in PromiseImpl.reject by allowing nullable code

labels.name: CLA Signed
labels.description: This label is managed by the Facebook bot. Authors need to sign the CLA before a PR can be reviewed.

labels.name: Shared with Meta
labels.description: Applied via automation to indicate that an Issue or Pull Request has been shared with the team.

prs.body:
# Fix NullPointerException in PromiseImpl.reject

## Summary

This PR fixes a `NullPointerException` that occurs when `Promise.reject` is called with a `null` code from Java native modules.

The issue arises because `Promise.kt` and `PromiseImpl.kt` defined the `code` parameter as non-nullable `String` in several overloads. However, `PromiseImpl`'s internal logic (specifically the catch-all `reject` method) is designed to handle `null` codes by defaulting to `EUNSPECIFIED`.

When a Java module (such as `react-native-ble-plx`) calls `promise.reject(null, message)`, Kotlin's generated null-checks throw a `NullPointerException` before the method body is executed.

## Changelog

[Android] [Fixed] - Allow nullable `code` in `Promise.reject` to prevent NPEs from Java modules

## Test Plan

1.  Create a native module in Java that calls `promise.reject(null, "Error message")`.
2.  Before this fix, the app crashes with `java.lang.NullPointerException: Parameter specified as non-null is null: method com.facebook.react.bridge.PromiseImpl.reject, parameter code`.
3.  After this fix, the promise is rejected with code `EUNSPECIFIED` and the app does not crash.

## Related Issue

Fixes #54722

prs.title: docs: Add computed fields guide and example (fixes #28378)
prs.body:
# Pull Request: Add Computed Fields Guide and Example

**Fixes #28378**

## Description

This PR adds a comprehensive guide and working example demonstrating how to implement database-level computed/generated columns with Prisma ORM. While Prisma doesn't yet support computed fields natively in the schema syntax (like `@db.generated(...)`), users can achieve this functionality using database features with manual migration editing.

## Problem Statement

Issue #28378 requested a simpler way to define computed fields directly in the Prisma schema, such as:

```prisma
model User {
  countryCode    String
  phone          String
  formattedPhone String @db.generated('countryCode' || ' ' || 'phone')
}
```

Currently, this syntax is not supported. This PR provides a practical guide showing users how to achieve the same result using database-level generated columns.

## Solution

This PR adds a new example in `examples/computed-fields-guide/` that:

1. **Documents the approach** - Comprehensive README explaining how to use `GENERATED ALWAYS AS` syntax
2. **Provides working code** - Complete SQLite example that runs out-of-the-box
3. **Shows real use cases** - Implements the exact scenarios from #28378:
   - Phone number formatting (`countryCode + phone`)
   - Full name concatenation (`firstName + lastName`)
   - Email domain extraction
   - Price calculations with tax
4. **Covers multiple databases** - SQL examples for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite
5. **Includes best practices** - Type safety, indexing, troubleshooting

## What's Included

### Files Added

- **README.md** (362 lines) - Comprehensive guide covering:
  - Quick start tutorial
  - Database-specific syntax
  - Complete examples
  - STORED vs VIRTUAL columns
  - Best practices and troubleshooting
  - References to #28378 for feature tracking

- **prisma/schema.prisma** - Example schema with:
  - `User` model (fullName, formattedPhone, emailDomain)
  - `Product` model (totalPrice calculation)
  - JSDoc comments documenting the generation expressions

- **prisma/migrations/20251129051933_init/migration.sql** - SQLite migration showing:
  - `GENERATED ALWAYS AS ... STORED` syntax
  - String concatenation for names and phone numbers
  - Numeric calculations for pricing

- **src/index.ts** (168 lines) - Demonstration script with 7 examples:
  1. Creating records with auto-computed fields
  2. Querying by computed fields
  3. Full-text search on computed fields
  4. Numeric computations
  5. Updates (computed fields auto-update)
  6. Read-only enforcement
  7. Aggregations and grouping

- **prisma/seed.ts** - Seed data showing computed fields in action
- **package.json** - Project configuration
- **.gitignore** - Ignores node_modules and database files
- **.env.example** - Example configuration
- **tsconfig.json** - TypeScript configuration

## How to Test

```bash
cd examples/computed-fields-guide
npm install
export DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
npx prisma migrate deploy
npm run dev
```

Expected output:
- ✅ All 7 examples execute successfully
- ✅ Computed fields automatically populated
- ✅ Queries by computed fields work correctly
- ✅ Attempt to write to computed field fails (as expected)

## Example Output

```
🚀 Demonstrating Computed Fields with Prisma
============================================================

📝 Example 1: Creating a User
------------------------------------------------------------
Input data:
  First Name: Alice
  Last Name: Johnson
  Country Code: +1
  Phone: 5559876543

Computed fields (automatically generated):
  ✨ Full Name: Alice Johnson
  ✨ Formatted Phone: +1 5559876543
  ✨ Email Domain: prisma.io

✅ All examples completed successfully!
```

## Benefits

1. **Solves the user's problem** - Provides a clear path to implement computed fields
2. **Reduces confusion** - Comprehensive documentation answers common questions
3. **Easy to test** - Uses SQLite, no Docker or external DB required
4. **Production-ready** - Shows best practices for all major databases
5. **Feature tracking** - References #28378 so users know native support is being tracked

## How This Addresses Issue #28378

The issue author wanted a built-in way to define computed fields in the schema. While native syntax support is not yet available, this guide:

- ✅ Shows the current best-practice workaround
- ✅ Implements the exact use case requested (phone number formatting)
- ✅ Makes the process straightforward with step-by-step instructions
- ✅ Provides a working example users can reference
- ✅ Documents the path forward (references #28378 for native syntax)

## Future Improvements

This example provides value today while we work toward native schema syntax. When Prisma adds support for something like `@db.generated(...)`, this guide can be updated to show both approaches.

## Screenshots

**Schema Definition:**
```prisma
model User {
  firstName      String
  lastName       String
  countryCode    String
  phone          String
  
  /// @generated ALWAYS AS (firstName || ' ' || lastName) STORED
  fullName       String?
  
  /// @generated ALWAYS AS (countryCode || ' ' || phone) STORED
  formattedPhone String?
}
```

**Migration SQL:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE "User" (
    "fullName" TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS ("firstName" || ' ' || "lastName") STORED,
    "formattedPhone" TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS ("countryCode" || ' ' || "phone") STORED
);
```

**Application Code:**
```typescript
const user = await prisma.user.create({
  data: {
    firstName: 'Alice',
    lastName: 'Johnson',
    countryCode: '+1',
    phone: '5559876543',
  },
})

console.log(user.fullName)         // "Alice Johnson"
console.log(user.formattedPhone)   // "+1 5559876543"
```

## Checklist

- [x] Tested on SQLite (included in example)
- [x] Documentation covers PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite
- [x] Code follows Prisma examples conventions
- [x] README.md is comprehensive and clear
- [x] Example runs successfully out-of-the-box
- [x] References issue #28378
- [x] Includes best practices and troubleshooting
- [x] All files properly formatted

## Related Links

- Issue: https://github.com/prisma/prisma/issues/28378
- PostgreSQL Generated Columns: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/ddl-generated-columns.html
- MySQL Generated Columns: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/create-table-generated-columns.html
- SQLite Generated Columns: https://www.sqlite.org/gencol.html

prs.body:
just tidied up your list — removed duplicates, grouped things properly under each topic, and made the format consistent so it’s easier to read. Basically, it’s the same content but more organized and clear.

## What does this PR do?
Add resource(s) | Remove resource(s) | Add info | Improve repo

## IMPORTANT
- [ ] Read our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/EbookFoundation/free-programming-books/blob/main/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ] Is this a revision of a previously submitted PR? If so, STOP! Go back, reopen the PR, and add commit(s) the branch you previously submitted. Please don't make the job of reviewing more difficult by hiding previous work.

## For resources
### Description

### Why is this valuable (or not)?

### How do we know it's really free?

### For book lists, is it a book? For course lists, is it a course? etc.

## Checklist:
- [ ] [Search](https://ebookfoundation.github.io/free-programming-books-search/) for duplicates.
- [ ] Include author(s) and platform where appropriate.
- [ ] Put lists in alphabetical order, correct spacing.
- [ ] Add needed indications (PDF, access notes, under construction).
- [ ] Used an informative name for this pull request.

## Follow-up

- Check the status of GitHub Actions and resolve any reported warnings!
